teaching_card.title: Area and Volume (Easy)
teaching_card.body: Area measures a flat region; volume measures space inside a solid. Rectangle area = base × height; triangle area = ½ × base × height; box volume = length × width × height.

screens.title: Area formulas
screens.body: Area measures the flat space inside a shape. Rectangle = base × height. Triangle = ½ × base × height (it's half a rectangle). Circle = πr². Match the formula to the shape in the figure.
key_points: Triangle: ½ × base × height.  Circle: πr².

screens.title: Volume formulas
screens.body: Volume measures the space inside a solid. Rectangular box = length × width × height. Cylinder = πr²h (base area × height). Volume always multiplies three lengths, so the units are cubic.

screens.title: Don't mix them up
screens.body: Common traps: using perimeter instead of area, forgetting the ½ for a triangle, using r instead of r² for circles, or stopping at the base area instead of multiplying by the height for volume.
key_points: Circles use r², not r; finish volume with × height.

screens.title: Try the moves
screens.body: A triangle with base 6 and height 8 has area ½·6·8 = 24. A cylinder with radius 3 and height 5 has volume π·3²·5 = 45π.
